summary: The provided evidence confirms that gut microbiota profiles are indeed altered by irradiation through documented cases of radiation-induced intestinal damage leading directly to detectable bacterial overgrowths and treatment strategies specifically aimed at restoring microbial balance. This contradicts initial skepticism regarding direct effects; instead, it establishes clear clinical correlations between radiotherapy and observable shifts in gastrointestinal microflora.
